title: AIDLC project configuration
description: Per-project configuration for the Bee-Logical Claude AIDLC framework. Lives at the workspace control plane in .claude/aidlc.config.json. Supports a single repo (mono, the default) or many repos (poly).

key.description: Uppercase work-item ID prefix, e.g. PROJ.

workspace.description: Workspace topology. Absent or layout=mono → single repo (the top-level git/stack/ux blocks apply). layout=poly → the repos[] array defines each git repo; this file sits at the workspace root (control plane) and the product repos are subfolders under root.

root.description: Directory, relative to this config's location, under which the declared repo paths live. Usually '.'.

crossRepoSplit.description: Poly only. The work-item tier at which cross-repo work is split into single-repo RUNNABLE units (one run = one repo = one branch = one PR — that leaf is always single-repo either way). 'story' (default, recommended): a Feature fans out to per-repo Stories; each Story is one repo and its Tasks are that repo's breakdown. Fits ADO's Epic→Feature→Story→Task hierarchy natively and makes one Story = one PR. 'task': a User Story is a cross-repo UMBRELLA that captures one unit of user value, and its child Tasks are the per-repo runnable units (API task → backend repo, UI task → frontend repo, migration task → db repo); the Story rolls up when its tasks complete. Both are supported; pick the one your ADO board is authored for. See aidlc:work-items → Cross-repo split tier.

architecture.description: The project's shape as DERIVED FROM REQUIREMENTS by /aidlc:bootstrap, rather than answered up front. init may write {status:pending} to defer topology/stack/repos to bootstrap (the greenfield-from-requirements flow); bootstrap then infers them, sets status=resolved, and writes workspace.layout + (stack or repos[]) + workspace.crossRepoSplit to match. A human who authored those fields directly can leave this absent (treated as resolved).

status.description: pending = init deferred the architecture; the topology/stack/repos in this file are placeholders and /aidlc:bootstrap will overwrite them from the requirements. resolved = the architecture has been determined (by bootstrap or a human) and workspace.layout/stack/repos are authoritative. Absent ⇒ resolved.

style.description: Architecture style bootstrap inferred from the requirements, biased to the SIMPLEST that fits (YAGNI). Drives the mono/poly + repos[] shape and crossRepoSplit: monolith / modular-monolith ⇒ mono (single repo, modules internal); microservices ⇒ poly (service-per-repo). Recorded for traceability.

resolvedBy.description: What set the architecture, e.g. "/aidlc:bootstrap" or "init" or "human".

rationale.description: One-line why, grounded in the requirements (the split/no-split signal bootstrap acted on).

repos.description: Declared git repos. Empty (or omitted) means mono — a single repo synthesized from the top-level git/stack/ux blocks. Non-empty means poly. When architecture.status=pending these are placeholders until /aidlc:bootstrap resolves them.

name.description: Short unique id used to route items and label PRs, e.g. backend.

path.description: Repo checkout path relative to workspace.root.

role.description: Free-text description used as a routing hint by the orchestrator.

mode.description: remote (default): push the branch and open a PR; a human merges it. local: no remote — after green verify the pipeline integrates via a confirmed local --no-ff merge into the default branch.

labels.description: Label hints: an item labeled with any of these routes to this repo.

ux.description: Per-repo UX config for frontend repos (overrides the top-level ux block for this repo).

default.description: The fallback repo when routing is otherwise ambiguous. At most one repo should set this.

git.description: Mono-mode single-repo git settings. Ignored when repos[] is non-empty (poly).

mode.description: remote (default): push the branch and open a PR; a human merges it. local: no remote — after green verify the pipeline integrates via a confirmed local --no-ff merge into the default branch.

stack.description: Mono-mode stack. In poly, each repo carries its own stack.

envFileAccess.description: Whether the pipeline may read or change env files (.env, .env.example, .env.local, …). "deny" (default): the env-guard hook hard-blocks every read/edit/write of an env file — they can carry secrets, and non-secret variable names/shape are meant to live in a human-maintained .env.example. "ask": the pipeline may touch env files, but EVERY read/change is surfaced to the user to approve or reject (the confirmation prompt shows the exact diff/content), keeping the human in the loop. Enforced by hooks/scripts/env-guard.mjs and fails closed (a missing/malformed config is treated as "deny"). This replaces the old static Read(.env*) deny rule, which could never be relaxed.

verification.description: Per-agent verification cadence — the pipeline's biggest recurring cost. Each agent runs on its own schedule so tokens are spent only where they earn it. Defaults are economical: reviewer/qa on-demand, security per-epic (confirmed). The deterministic CI gate (lint/format/typecheck/boundaries/tests) runs regardless and is the per-item floor.

mode.description: auto: agent cadences decide. manual: skip all agents, human reviews the PR. ask: prompt per item.

reviewer.description: Adversarial diff review vs AC + coding standards.

qa.description: Full suite + missing-test authoring. (Bug repro-first still runs at implement.)

security.description: Deep security pass. risk-based = per-item only when the diff is risky.

securityConfirm.description: Ask the user to confirm before running the security pass.

ux.description: Mono-mode / default UX config. In poly, each frontend repo may carry its own ux block.

pluginFeedback.description: Plugin self-feedback (dogfood) channel. When enabled, the pipeline records AIDLC-plugin friction it hits during a run — gaps, wrong/missing guidance, steps it had to work around, skill/agent/template bugs (distinct from project bugs) — as structured entries in a local inbox, for the plugin maintainer to triage. Default off, so normal projects stay quiet; a project used to dogfood the plugin turns it on. See the aidlc:dogfood skill.

enabled.description: Turn on plugin self-feedback capture for this workspace.

inbox.description: Path (relative to the control plane) of the append-only feedback inbox the pipeline writes to.
